must_find.description: Single function run_pipeline_for_issue handles 6 distinct concerns (validation, git ops, phase execution, budget tracking, review handling, PR creation/notification) in ~170 lines, violating single responsibility principle
Function takes 8 parameters including boolean flags (notify_slack) and optional values (slack_webhook, github_repo) that control internal branching — a sign the function should be decomposed
Function mixes high-level orchestration (phase iteration, review coordination) with low-level operations (git command execution, HTTP webhook posting, string slugification)

must_not_flag.description: Using async/await for git commands and HTTP calls is correct — the issue is the function's scope, not its async nature
The early-return error handling pattern with bail! and context() is idiomatic Rust and not itself an architectural problem
